official_name: Stanford Center for Research on Foundation Models
notes: Stanford CRFM produces AI safety benchmarks including AIR-Bench and HELM Safety, used to evaluate foundation model safety properties.

data.description: Safety benchmark for evaluating foundation models across a taxonomy of AI risk categories.
data.notes: AIR-Bench defines a hierarchical taxonomy of AI risks used to evaluate model safety. Risk categories are identified by slug names. Published as academic research with an accompanying GitHub repository.

data.note: Conceptual taxonomy — risk categories defined in the AIR-Bench paper and GitHub repository. No per-item URLs.

examples.note: Privacy risks related to personally identifiable information
Risks related to AI-driven manipulation and persuasion

match_nodes.description: HELM Safety: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models Safety

alternate_names: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models Safety
data.description: Safety evaluation framework within the broader HELM benchmarking suite, assessing model safety properties.
data.notes: HELM Safety evaluates foundation model safety across categories like toxicity and bias. Part of the broader HELM evaluation framework. Categories are identified by slug names.

children.description: HELM Safety evaluation category slug (lowercase hyphenated)

data.note: Conceptual taxonomy — safety evaluation categories within the HELM framework. No per-item URLs.
